figures.caption: Table 1. Comparisons with state-of-the-arts on the 3DSSG dataset. The inference model backbones are denoted by symbols: “pn” for PointNet and “pt” for PointTransformer. “LFS”: Learning from scratch. “PointDif ”: Weights of object encoder initialized with PointDif (Zheng et al., 2024). “ToLL”: Weights of complete 3DSG encoders initialized with our ToLL. “+MLP only”: Only MLP heads are fine-tuned. “RP”: Weights of complete 3DSG backbone initialized with random parameters.

Table 2. Detailed performance on Predicate Classification (Head, Body, Tail) and Triplet Prediction (Zero-Shot setting). “pn” for PointNet and “pt” for PointTransformer. “LFS”: Learning from scratch. “ToLL”: Weights of complete 3DSG backbone initialized with our ToLL.
Table 5. Hyperparameters for SwAV, Queues, and Prototypes.

Table 3. Ablation Studies on 3DSSG. We analyze the impact of Anchor-Conditioned Topological Geometric Reasoning (ACTGR), Structural Multi-view Augmentation (SMA), initialization strategies, and cross-modal loss. The best results in each block are highlighted.
Figure 2. Our 3D Scene Graph Pretraining scheme via Topological Layout Learning with Structural Multi-view Augmentation.
Figure 4. Visualization of latent features clustering.

Figure 9. Qualitative Results of Our Method vs. Baseline (Learning From Scratch) & OCRL. The columns from left to right represent: the input Point Cloud, the scene graph generated by SGFN (w/o ToLL) with learning from scratch, OCRL, and Ours (SGFN w/ ToLL) . Green nodes indicate correctly identified objects or detailed spatial relationships, while red nodes/edges in baselines often represent misclassifications or over-simplified relations.

Figure 8. Visualization of Clustering Metrics Evolution. The curves demonstrate the progressive improvement of NMI, ARI, and ACC scores for both Edge (Relation) and Object semantic recovery. The metrics were recorded at 10-epoch intervals over a total of 150 pre-training epochs, compared against the “learning from the scratch (LFS)” baseline. Ours “Pre-training” results is test without any fine-tuning. “LFS” results are obtained by training the same architecture from scratch on the 3DSSG dataset with supervised labels.
